title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · Recovery Case: Integration Policy
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git integration policy increment in a team contribution and review protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
Review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Integration Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Integration Policy contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

content.text: Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Integration Policy condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

hints: Name the Integration Policy precondition or invariant that the theory case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.

options.text: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change. This does not resolve predict integration policy.

For predict integration policy, apply Integration Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the a team contribution and review protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Integration Policy for a team contribution and review protocol.
Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Integration Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. Competency probe: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Integration Policy application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

content.text: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Integration Policy while producing a team contribution and review protocol.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. Competency probe: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently. A defensible solution applies Integration Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a team contribution and review protocol.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-integration-policy — Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
# Evidence: changed Git case 5
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change. This fails the Integration Policy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Integration Policy evidence

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Integration Policy into observable performance evidence.

lines.label: required

options.text: For repair the integration policy defect, apply Integration Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the a team contribution and review protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. steps.title: Defend the Integration Policy model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Integration Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. Competency probe: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
steps.why: Retrieving Integration Policy through the concrete a team contribution and review protocol case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
